blocks.heading: A season-long fantasy golf game — pick one PGA Tour golfer a week, earn his real prize money, never reuse a golfer — built as a Next.js + tRPC monolith whose heartbeat is a 15-minute ESPN sync cron.

rows.value: PPGL (Perfect Pick Golf League, [ppgl.app](https://ppgl.app)): private leagues with PayPal checkout, a season planner for future-week prepicks, live projected earnings during tournaments, and commissioner + admin override surfaces. One Vercel deployment, one Postgres (Neon).

A strict three-layer server — **routers** (thin, validate → service) → **services** (business logic, `withTransaction`) → **repositories** (the only layer that touches `db`) — under a Next.js App Router front. Eight domains: five product slices, a data layer, an ops surface, and the shared shell.

The weekly loop: a user plans a PLANNED pick → the cron locks it when the tournament starts (field + not-yet-used validation) → live sync writes projected earnings all weekend → completion promotes real money and triggers the earnings rollup → standings re-rank.

primer.p: `GET /api/cron/sync` runs every 15 minutes and drives *every* game-state transition: it locks PLANNED picks at tee-off, projects live earnings, promotes final results, kicks the earnings rollup, runs bot auto-picks, sends pick reminders, and cleans up abandoned leagues. Nothing else moves the game clock.

primer.h: Picks are soft until they're hard.
primer.p: A PLANNED pick is a free-floating intention — movable, deletable, not consuming the golfer. Only the LOCKED status burns a golfer for the season. Reads *overlay* the lock the moment `startDate` passes; the cron makes it real in the database shortly after.

`resolveLeagueMemberId` + `assertLeagueActive` gate every gameplay mutation, and the `leagueMemberId` they return is the key picks, standings, and earnings all hang off. Everyone is lazily auto-enrolled in the global league on first contact, so the guard doubles as onboarding.

`LeagueMemberSeason` is a materialized rollup: every trigger recomputes a member's whole season from one SQL aggregate over LOCKED picks (plus commissioner adjustments) and overwrites the row. There is no ledger to drift.

primer.h: Auth checks live in exactly one place.
primer.p: The three-rung procedure ladder in `src/server/trpc.ts` — `publicProcedure` → `protectedProcedure` → `adminProcedure` — is the only authorization boundary; page-level redirects are UX, not security.

blocks.title: The domain map
blocks.intro: Five product slices (`game`, `leagues`, `scoring`, `accounts`, `payments`) over a data layer (`golf-data`) and the shared shell (`web-shell`), with `admin` as an operator lens that writes into all of them. Arrows are the load-bearing runtime edges — every domain additionally rides web-shell's tRPC/db plumbing (those edges are omitted for legibility), and `admin` reaches into every slice.

diagram.mermaid:
graph TD
  espn["ESPN APIs"]
  paypal["PayPal"]
  golfdata["golf-data · ESPN sync"]
  game["game · picks + planner"]
  leagues["leagues · lifecycle"]
  scoring["scoring · rollup + standings"]
  accounts["accounts · auth + email"]
  payments["payments · checkout + promos"]
  admin["admin · back-office"]
  webshell["web-shell · trpc + db + chrome"]
  golfdata -->|polls 15m| espn
  golfdata -->|locks picks| game
  golfdata -->|rollup| scoring
  golfdata -->|reminders| accounts
  golfdata -->|cleanup| leagues
  game -->|schedule| golfdata
  game -->|member guards| leagues
  game -->|recalc| scoring
  scoring -->|membership| leagues
  leagues -->|checkout| payments
  payments --> paypal
  payments -->|webhook confirm| leagues
  admin -->|sync ops| golfdata
  admin -->|rollup ops| scoring
  classDef changed fill:#ffd43b,stroke:#f08c00,color:#1b1b1b,stroke-width:2px;
  classDef external fill:#e9ecef,stroke:#868e96,color:#1b1b1b;
  classDef store fill:#e5dbff,stroke:#9775fa,color:#1b1b1b;
  class golfdata changed;
  class espn,paypal external;
  class webshell store;

legend.label: The heartbeat — its cron drives every game-state transition

diagram.caption: `golf-data` is highlighted because its 15-minute cron is the game clock: pick locking, live money, finals, rollups, reminders, and league cleanup all fire from it. `payments` is the one domain not mounted on the tRPC root router — PayPal runs over plain API routes.

blocks.title: The weekly loop
blocks.intro: The one flow worth memorizing — what happens to a single pick across a tournament week. Everything left of the lock is user intent; everything right of it is machine-driven by the sync cron.

diagram.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  user(["member"]) -->|plan / submit| plan["PLANNED pick (soft reservation)"]
  plan -->|tournament starts| lock["cron locks at tee-off: field + unused validation"]
  lock -->|all weekend| live["live sync: projected earnings"]
  live -->|tournament completes| final["results promoted: real prize money"]
  final --> rollup["season rollup: recompute + overwrite"]
  rollup --> standings[("standings")]
  classDef changed fill:#ffd43b,stroke:#f08c00,color:#1b1b1b,stroke-width:2px;
  class lock changed;
diagram.caption: A stale PLANNED pick (golfer not in field, or already used) is *not* burned — it's left behind and the member simply earns $0 that week, per the league's `burnStalePicks` policy. Missed weeks are the absence of a row, not a status.

blocks.title: The 8 domains
blocks.intro: Each domain owns one slice of the product. Every tile links to a full deep-dive page: what it owns, how its pieces wire up, what it exposes, and what it depends on.

tiles.name: game
tiles.purpose: The product core: weekly pick CRUD, the season planner (PLANNED prepicks), bot auto-picks, and the dashboard. Owns the `Pick` model and its one-pick-per-week unique constraint.

tiles.name: golf-data
tiles.purpose: The one-way clock: a 15-minute cron pulls ESPN schedule/field/leaderboard data through a provider-agnostic interface and drives every game-state transition. Owns tournaments, golfers, results.

tiles.name: leagues
tiles.purpose: League lifecycle — create → checkout → invite → join → approval — plus commissioner tools with an append-only audit log. Membership *is* the app's permission system.

tiles.name: scoring
tiles.purpose: The money engine: converts LOCKED picks × tournament results into season totals via a recompute-and-overwrite rollup, and serves every leaderboard and standings view.

tiles.name: accounts
tiles.purpose: Identity and comms: one Better Auth instance behind a catch-all route (sessions in the app's own Postgres), settings, and the cron-driven pick-reminder email pipeline.

tiles.name: payments
tiles.purpose: The only money-in path: PayPal checkout for private leagues with a webhook backstop, and promo codes (free bypass + partial discounts). No orders table — the `League` row is the ledger.

tiles.name: admin
tiles.purpose: The operator back-office: 35 `adminProcedure`-gated procedures over tournaments, results, users, golfers, leagues, and the cron console. Owns no models — a write-everywhere lens.

tiles.name: web-shell
tiles.purpose: The pipe, not the water: the tRPC procedure ladder (the app's only auth boundary), the Kysely db core with `withTransaction`, and the chrome every page shares.
